mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 5% signals weakening labor market conditions, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high UNRATE regimes exceed 1.5% in 60% of cases, or the correlation between UNRATE and returns drops below -0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in industrial production and rising oil prices signals supply-side bottlenecks, which drive inflation expectations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in supply shock scenarios exceed 2% in 50% of cases, or the interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL shows no significant impact on returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in residential construction permits combined with declining consumer confidence signals strong demand fundamentals, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in high HOUST/low RSAFS scenarios fall below 0.5% in 40% of cases, or the interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS shows no significant impact on returns (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the consumer price index below 2% signals disinflationary pressures, which reduces real interest rate expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in low CPIAUCSL regimes fall below 0.3% in 50% of cases, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and returns drops below 0.15 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls above 150k monthly signals strong labor market dynamics, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high PAYEMS regimes fall below 1.0% in 40% of cases, or the correlation between PAYEMS and returns drops below 0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP growth below 2% signals economic contraction, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in low GDPC1 regimes exceed 1.5% in 50% of cases, or the correlation between GDPC1 and returns drops below -0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production below 100 combined with rising commodity prices signals supply-side disruptions, which drive inflation and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply crisis scenarios exceed 1.0% in 40% of cases, or the interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL shows no significant impact on returns (p > 0.1).
